cocoon plugin scaffold — create a new <id>/ directory under the project plugins overlay

By default the new directory is created under <workspace>/.cocoon/plugins/<id>/,
auto-discovered from the nearest cocoon.toml. Pass --plugins-dir <path> to
override (the path is taken as-is, joined with <id>).

The new directory contains a plugin.toml declaring a single
[install.methods.<category>] entry and an install.<category>.sh skeleton
matching the chosen template — installer (curl|bash), binary (single
binary download), apt (apt repo / .deb), or archive (multi-file
extract). With --with-install-user a second install_user.sh hook is
emitted (kept plugin-scoped, not per-method).

Usage:
  cocoon plugin scaffold <id> [flags]

Flags:
      --default              mark plugin enabled by default
      --description string   short description (no URL)
      --force                overwrite <plugins-dir>/<id>/ if it already exists
  -h, --help                 help for scaffold
      --name string          display name (e.g. "GitHub CLI")
      --non-interactive      skip interactive prompts; require all fields above
      --plugins-dir string   output directory (default: <workspace>/.cocoon/plugins, auto-discovered from the config file)
      --requires-root        install script runs as root
      --template string      install method category: installer | binary | apt | archive (catalog-canonical names — see docs/plugins.md) (default "installer")
      --url string           upstream URL (e.g. https://github.com/owner/repo)
      --version-capable      generate $PIN / $CHECKSUM_* boilerplate
      --with-install-user    also generate install_user.sh
